person.name: Wojciech Sacewicz
person.headline: AI-Native Developer and Product Engineer
person.summary: Wojciech Sacewicz is an AI-native software developer and product engineer based in Poland / Tricity. He builds React and TypeScript products, Cloudflare services, internal tools, automation workflows and software with coding agents, review and verification in the loop.
person.fit: Best suited to product engineering, AI-assisted software development, full-stack web work, internal tools, workflow automation and teams adopting coding agents without lowering engineering standards.
person.education: Computer Science and Econometrics at the University of Gdańsk

capabilityGroups.title: AI-native development
items: OpenAI Codex and Claude Code workflows
Tests, browser verification and production builds

caseStudies.title: Veldia — shift management SaaS and PWA
caseStudies.description: A mobile-first product for small shift-based businesses, covering schedules, hours, availability, roles, onboarding, pricing, trial and AI-assisted schedule drafting.
caseStudies.problem: Small shift-based businesses often coordinate schedules and work hours through paper, chat messages and fragmented spreadsheets.
caseStudies.ownership: Product positioning, interface design, React implementation, schedule and hour workflows, onboarding, pricing, trial, SEO and an AI-assisted schedule draft reviewed by a manager.
caseStudies.outcome: A live mobile-first SaaS/PWA with real operational workflows rather than a portfolio-only prototype.

caseStudies.title: llmpolska — practical AI platform for Poland
caseStudies.description: A Polish community, tools and education platform focused on practical AI work, product discovery and useful implementation knowledge.
caseStudies.problem: Polish AI practitioners need a focused place for practical tools, education and community rather than another generic AI news site.
caseStudies.ownership: Product positioning, brand, navigation, React Router implementation, Cloudflare delivery and AI-tool discovery surfaces.
caseStudies.outcome: A live server-rendered platform that combines engineering, distribution and product positioning.

caseStudies.title: DOVISTA — SAP reporting automation with UiPath
caseStudies.description: An SAP-related reporting workflow mapped, implemented and deployed with UiPath, Document Understanding and OCR.
caseStudies.problem: A purchasing-report workflow required repetitive manual processing and exposed the team to avoidable data-entry errors.
caseStudies.ownership: Process mapping with stakeholders, UiPath implementation, SAP workflow integration, OCR and Document Understanding, deployment and management-facing adoption material.
caseStudies.outcome: Report generation time was reduced by 40% while manual data-entry risk was lowered.

experience.summary: Commercial engineering across backend services, web features and developer workflows, with practical responsibility for coherent delivery, quality and security.
Owned an SAP-related reporting automation from process mapping through deployment, reducing report generation time by 40%.
Built operational discipline through repeatable verification, documentation and clear communication before moving fully into engineering.

faq.question: What kind of developer is Wojciech Sacewicz?
faq.answer: An early-career AI-native software developer and product engineer who works across React and TypeScript products, backend-connected web features, Cloudflare delivery, internal tools and workflow automation.

faq.question: Where is Wojciech Sacewicz based?
faq.answer: Poland / Tricity, with experience collaborating in commercial teams and suitability for local, hybrid or remote European work.

faq.question: How does he use AI coding agents?
faq.answer: Across repository research, requirement clarification, implementation, refactoring, debugging, review and documentation, followed by tests, browser verification and production-build checks.

faq.question: What evidence shows product and engineering ownership?
faq.answer: Live products including Veldia and llmpolska, commercial work at IDEGO and a deployed DOVISTA automation that reduced report generation time by 40%.

faq.question: What roles are the strongest match?
faq.answer: AI-native developer, junior software engineer, product engineer, React or TypeScript developer, full-stack web developer, internal-tools developer and automation engineer.
